name: Type Theory — Normalization
description: Normal form: no β-redex. Strong normalization: every typable term reduces to normal form. Curry-Howard: types as propositions, terms as proofs.
mermaid:
graph TD
    D1["Def: Normal form\nno β-redex"]
    D2["Def: Weakly normalizing\nsome red to nf"]
    D3["Def: Strongly normalizing\nall red to nf"]
    D4["Def: Curry-Howard\nA type = A prop"]
    T1["Thm: STLC strongly\nnormalizing"]
    T2["Thm: Proof = term\nProp = type"]
    D1 --> D2
    D2 --> D3
    D3 --> T1
    D4 --> T2
    T1 --> T2
    classDef definition fill:#b197fc,color:#fff,stroke:#9775fa
    classDef theorem fill:#51cf66,color:#fff,stroke:#40c057
    class D1,D2,D3,D4 definition
    class T1,T2 theorem
